name: build_phase1_registries
outcome_goal: Create the three registry tables, app manifest table, and seed data in Postgres

jobs.label: build_registry_schema
jobs.prompt:
You are working at the Praxis repository root. The platform uses Postgres via $WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL. All config is in Postgres, no JSON files.

Create a migration file at: Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/016_app_builder_registries.sql

It must create these tables:

1. registry_ui_components — UI primitives registry
   - id text PRIMARY KEY
   - name text UNIQUE NOT NULL
   - description text NOT NULL
   - category text NOT NULL (e.g., 'input', 'display', 'layout', 'action')
   - props_schema jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}' (JSONSchema of configurable props)
   - emits_events jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]' (event names this component emits)
   - accepts_slots boolean DEFAULT false (can contain other components?)
   - default_size jsonb DEFAULT '{}' (default width/height hints)
   - search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (to_tsvector('english', coalesce(name,'') || ' ' || coalesce(description,''))) STORED
   - created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()

2. registry_calculations — Pre-built calculation/data primitives
   - id text PRIMARY KEY
   - name text UNIQUE NOT NULL
   - description text NOT NULL
   - category text NOT NULL (e.g., 'aggregation', 'dataset', 'algorithm', 'formula')
   - input_schema jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'
   - output_schema jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'
   - execution_type text NOT NULL (e.g., 'sql', 'python', 'platform_algo', 'mcp_tool')
   - resource_ref text (pointer to actual query/script/tool)
   - search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (to_tsvector('english', coalesce(name,'') || ' ' || coalesce(description,''))) STORED
   - created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()

3. registry_workflows — Pre-built workflow primitives
   - id text PRIMARY KEY
   - name text UNIQUE NOT NULL
   - description text NOT NULL
   - category text NOT NULL (e.g., 'research', 'coding', 'personal', 'automation', 'collaboration')
   - trigger_type text NOT NULL (e.g., 'manual', 'scheduled', 'webhook', 'event')
   - input_schema jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'
   - output_schema jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'
   - steps jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]' (workflow step definitions)
   - mcp_tool_refs jsonb DEFAULT '[]' (which MCP tools this workflow uses)
   - search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (to_tsvector('english', coalesce(name,'') || ' ' || coalesce(description,''))) STORED
   - created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()

4. app_manifests — User-created app specifications
   - id text PRIMARY KEY
   - name text NOT NULL
   - description text DEFAULT ''
   - created_by text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'system'
   - intent_history jsonb DEFAULT '[]' (chat history of how this app was built)
   - manifest jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}' (the actual app spec: state + layout + bindings)
   - version integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
   - parent_manifest_id text (for version chains)
   - status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' (draft, active, archived)
   - search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (to_tsvector('english', coalesce(name,'') || ' ' || coalesce(description,''))) STORED
   - created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()
   - updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()

5. app_manifest_history — Append-only version history for undo/redo
   - id text PRIMARY KEY
   - manifest_id text NOT NULL REFERENCES app_manifests(id)
   - version integer NOT NULL
   - manifest_snapshot jsonb NOT NULL
   - change_description text DEFAULT ''
   - changed_by text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'system' (who made this change: 'user', 'ai', 'system')
   - created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()

Add GIN indexes on all search_vector columns. Add indexes on manifest_id + version.

Then apply the migration by connecting to Postgres and executing it.

After creating the tables, seed them with initial data:

UI Components (seed all from the wireframe):
- drag_and_drop_canvas: Drag and drop interface with snapping (category: layout)
- text_box: Text input field (category: input)
- table: Data table with sorting and filtering (category: display)
- icon: Icon selector and display (category: display)
- box: Container box with configurable borders and padding (category: layout)
- color_scheme: Color scheme selector and applicator (category: display)
- chart: Chart component - bar, line, pie, area (category: display)
- text_format: Rich text with formatting options (category: display)
- button: Clickable action button with variants (category: action)
- dropdown: Dropdown selector with search and multi-select (category: input)

Pre-built Calculations (seed all from the wireframe):
- schema_charts_rollups: Pre-built schema charts and data rollups (category: aggregation)
- platform_datasets: Links to platform datasets and tables (category: dataset)
- platform_algos: Platform algorithms - scoring, ranking, classification (category: algorithm)
- common_calcs: Common calculations - averages, sums, percentages, growth rates (category: formula)

Pre-built Workflows (seed all from the wireframe):
- brainstorming: Collaborative brainstorming with AI assistance (category: personal, trigger: manual)
- research: Research workflow - gather, analyze, synthesize (category: research, trigger: manual)
- coding_workflow: Code generation, review, and testing pipeline (category: coding, trigger: manual)
- personal_workflow: Personal task and habit workflows (category: personal, trigger: manual)
- collateral_creation: Document and content creation pipeline (category: collaboration, trigger: manual)
- hitl: Human-in-the-loop approval and review (category: collaboration, trigger: event)
- schedule_based: Recurring scheduled task execution (category: automation, trigger: scheduled)
- triggers_and_actions: Event-driven trigger-action automations (category: automation, trigger: webhook)
- workflow_composition: Workflows that call and orchestrate other workflows (category: automation, trigger: manual)
- data_splitting_logging: Data partitioning, routing, and audit logging (category: automation, trigger: event)
- handoff_mechanisms: Task handoff between humans and AI agents (category: collaboration, trigger: event)
- instructions_by_tool: Tool-specific instruction sets and configurations (category: coding, trigger: manual)

Write the SQL file, apply it, verify all tables exist and have the correct row counts.
